title: Scheduled Restore Test Outcomes vs Policy Frequency

compliance.assurance_rationale: CSF 2.0 RC.RP-01 (Recovery plan execution) — UC monitors recovery plan execution and restoration activities, providing partial evidence that the recovery portion of the incident response plan is executed.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF RC.RP-01 (Recovery plan execution) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.46: Scheduled Restore Test Outcomes vs Policy Frequency.

| stats latest(status) as last_status, latest(_time) as last_run by policy_name

| where days_since>90 OR last_status!="success"
description: Tracks restore test jobs for recovery plan execution evidence beyond daily backup success.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes backup and sourcetypes cohesity:restore_test. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We track restore test jobs for recovery plan execution evidence beyond daily backup success — so you can show exactly what is being watched and when something went wrong.
implementation: (1) Align to RTO tiers; (2) document failures; (3) executive attestation. Indexes required: `index=backup`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cohesity:restore_test`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cohesity or Commvault.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `cohesity:restore_test`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Align to RTO tiers; (2) document failures; (3) executive attestation.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=backup sourcetype=cohesity:restore_test earliest=-365d
| stats latest(status) as last_status, latest(_time) as last_run by policy_name
| eval days_since=(now()-last_run)/86400
| where days_since>90 OR last_status!="success"
```

Understanding this SPL

**Scheduled Restore Test Outcomes vs Policy Frequency (RC.RP-01)** — Tracks restore test jobs for recovery plan execution evidence beyond daily backup success.

Documented **Data sources**: `cohesity:restore_test`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cohesity or Commvault. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: backup; **sourcetype**: cohesity:restore_test. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=backup, sourcetype=cohesity:restore_test, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by policy_name** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **days_since** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where days_since>90 OR last_status!="success"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table, Single value
knownFalsePositives: Identification gaps during new asset onboarding, M&A integration, or after refresh waves.
